channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Man in Manitoba cleared as judge rules trial unfair due to Crown misconduct
item.description: A Manitoba Court of King's Bench judge has ordered a stay of proceedings for a man accused of killing his mother when he was 16, citing serious breaches of his Charter rights to a fair trial. The accu...

item.title: ICPC arraigns former Kaduna governor El-Rufai over corruption charges
item.description: Security tight well well for Federal High Court for Kaduna as former Kaduna State Governor, Nasir El-Rufai, waka enter court for arraignment over corruption wahala. From as early as 7:00 a.m., DSS, Ni...

item.title: Raphael Tuju Investigated Over Alleged Staged Disappearance in Kenya
item.description: The Directorate of Criminal Investigations (DCI) has intensified its probe into former Cabinet Secretary Raphael Tuju over claims of a staged disappearance, potentially breaching Section 129 of the Pe...

item.title: Police and Security Teams Bust Major Vehicle Theft Operations in KZN and Gauteng
item.description: Law enforcement, working hand-in-hand with private security, has successfully disrupted major vehicle theft syndicates in KwaZulu-Natal and Gauteng. In Verulam, Durban, police arrested two suspects af...

item.title: Practical advice for thriving as a working mother
item.description: This article talks about the challenges and adjustments that come with being a working mother. Many women who are high performers struggle because they try to apply the same perfectionist standards th...

item.title: Sindh authorities mobilise ahead of expected rain from March 25
item.description: The Sindh government has issued a high alert across the province as the Pakistan Meteorological Department (PMD) forecasts another spell of rainfall from March 25 to March 29, with occasional breaks. ...

item.title: Colombian Military Plane Crash Near Puerto Leguízamo Leaves at Least 64 Dead
item.description: At least 64 people lost their lives after a Colombian military C-130 Hercules aircraft crashed shortly after takeoff from Caucaya Airport in southern Colombia, near Puerto Leguízamo, Putumayo. The pla...

item.title: Philippine airport fees set to decrease from April 1
item.description: Starting April 1, 2026, travelers in the Philippines will enjoy lower airport fees, as announced by aviation authorities. This reduction applies to terminal fees and other airport-related charges, aim...

item.title: Australia’s diesel supply is stable for now, but heavy reliance poses risks
item.description: Australia currently has adequate diesel supplies, with shipments arriving from countries including India, Malaysia, Singapore, and South Korea, keeping short-term stocks stable. Diesel is critical for...

item.title: Air India flight from Bengaluru diverted to Jeddah as a precaution over technical issue
item.description: An Air India flight, AI133, travelling from Bengaluru to London Heathrow was diverted to Jeddah, Saudi Arabia, on 23 March due to a suspected technical fault. Operated by a Boeing 787-8 Dreamliner, th...

item.title: How Strategic Oil Reserves Are Being Used to Stabilize Prices Amid the Iran War
item.description: In response to the Iran war and the closure of the Strait of Hormuz, which has cut off about 20% of the world's oil supply, the International Energy Agency announced the largest coordinated release of...

item.title: Panic Fuel Buying in SA Risks Supply and Price Stability
item.description: Recent reports of long queues at South African filling stations and bulk purchasing of diesel highlight a recurring issue: panic-driven behaviour in response to global uncertainties. The underlying co...

item.title: Japan joins PH-US Balikatan 2026 as combat troops participate for the first time
item.description: For the first time, Japan’s Self-Defense Forces will send combat troops to join the annual Balikatan military exercises between the Philippines and the United States in 2026. This announcement was mad...

item.title: Tanzania Urged to Use Weather Forecasts to Stay Safe During Wet Season
item.description: In Tanzania, people have been advised to pay close attention to weather forecasts as the rainy season spreads across various regions. Dr Ladislaus Chang'a, the Director General of the Tanzania Meteoro...

item.title: Delhi CM Rekha Gupta Presents ₹1.03 Lakh Crore Budget Highlighting 10 Key Initiatives
item.description: Delhi Chief Minister Rekha Gupta presented the state budget for 2026-27 in the Delhi Assembly with an outlay of ₹1.03 lakh crore, focusing on ten major areas. The budget aims to combine welfare, infra...

item.title: How Kenya can stop wasteful use of public funds and save more for development
item.description: The article warns that the Kenyan government is spending too much of its money in a way that does not help ordinary citizens, urging leaders to cut wasteful expenditure and use public funds more wisel...

item.title: Kelvin Kimutai Recovering After Serious Illness in Dubai
item.description: Kelvin Kimutai, a young Kenyan man who had travelled to Dubai to work and support his family, recently went through a frightening health scare. He fell seriously ill shortly after starting his new job...

item.title: Key African Headlines: Kenya, Nigeria, Zimbabwe, Rwanda, and Health Developments
item.description: This news roundup from AllAfrica covers several key stories from across the continent as of March 24, 2026. In Kenya, former Foreign Minister Raphael Tuju was arrested after allegedly staging his own ...

item.title: Supreme Court finds bias in army evaluation process harmed women officers' Permanent Commission chances
item.description: The Supreme Court has held that the denial of Permanent Commission (PC) to women Short Service Commission (SSC) officers in the armed forces was rooted in a flawed and discriminatory evaluation system...

item.title: EU pledges €372 million to support Nigeria’s agriculture and digital growth under Global Gateway plan
item.description: The European Union don announce one big €372 million support package to boost Nigeria’s agriculture, digital infrastructure, and general development under their Global Gateway initiative. The new fund...
